channel.title: Factorio Science Packs — Complete Recipes & Ratios Guide on Factorio Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Factorio Science Packs — Complete Recipes & Ratios Guide on Factorio Guide

item.title: Factorio Blue Science Guide - Chemical Science Pack Setup and Ratios
item.description:
Blue science is the first real complexity spike in Factorio. It's not just about adding a new ingredient -- it introduces fluids, cracking, and multi-step production chains. I've seen countless factory tours stall right here because the oil setup wasn't thought through.

TL;DR: Blue science needs oil processing for plastic and sulfur. Build 2 refineries, 1 sulfur plant, 2 plastic plants, and 5 science assemblers. The bottleneck is almost always petroleum throughput.

Recipe and Requirements

Each chemical science pack needs three components:

item.title: Factorio Red Science Guide - Automation Science Pack (First Science)
item.description:
You just placed your first assembling machine and fed it some iron plates, but the lab's been empty for 10 minutes and you're still hand-crafting research packs. I made the same mistake -- thinking one assembler of red science would carry me through the first hour. It won't. Here's what actually works.

TL;DR: 5-10 red science assemblers fed by a dedicated gear sub-factory. Use a splitter to give iron plates to both the gear assembler and the science line. Belt the finished science to 10+ labs in a row. That's it -- red science is your warmup.

The Quirky Recipe Nobody Explains

Red science (automation science pack) takes two ingredients: iron gear wheels and iron plates. One pack takes 5 seconds in an un-moduled assembling machine 1.
